title: Blanching
description: Blanching briefly boils vegetables or fruits then shocks them in ice water to preserve color and texture.

sections.title: Step 1
sections.content: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil.

sections.title: Step 2
sections.content: Add vegetables or fruits and cook for 1 to 3 minutes.

sections.title: Step 3
sections.content: Transfer immediately to an ice bath.

title: Boiling
description: Boiling cooks food in rapidly bubbling water at high temperature.

sections.title: Step 1
sections.content: Fill a pot with water and add salt.

sections.title: Step 2
sections.content: Bring water to a full rolling boil.

sections.title: Step 3
sections.content: Add ingredients and maintain vigorous bubbles.

title: Braising
description: Braising combines searing and slow cooking in liquid to tenderize tough cuts.

sections.title: Step 1
sections.content: Season and sear meat until browned.

sections.title: Step 3
sections.content: Cover and cook slowly at low heat.

title: Sautéing
description: Sautéing quickly cooks food in a small amount of fat over high heat.

sections.title: Step 1
sections.content: Heat skillet with oil or butter.

sections.title: Step 2
sections.content: Add food in a single layer.

title: Roasting
description: Roasting cooks food using hot dry air in an oven.

sections.title: Step 2
sections.content: Season ingredients with oil and spices.

sections.title: Step 3
sections.content: Spread food on a roasting pan.

title: Searing
description: Searing uses intense heat to create a browned crust on food.

sections.title: Step 2
sections.content: Add oil with high smoke point.

sections.title: Step 3
sections.content: Place meat or vegetables in pan.

sections.title: Step 4
sections.content: Cook without moving until crust forms.

title: Grilling
description: Grilling cooks food over direct radiant heat.

title: Steaming
description: Steaming cooks food using hot vapor from boiling water.

sections.title: Step 3
sections.content: Cover and allow steam to cook food.

title: Simmering
description: Simmering cooks food in liquid just below boiling temperature.

sections.title: Step 2
sections.content: Reduce heat until gentle bubbles appear.

sections.title: Step 4
sections.content: Cook slowly for even flavor development.

title: Frying
description: Frying cooks food in hot oil to create a crispy surface.

sections.title: Step 1
sections.content: Heat oil in pan or fryer.

sections.title: Step 4
sections.content: Drain excess oil on paper towel.

title: Deglazing
description: Deglazing dissolves caramelized bits left in a pan to create sauce.

sections.title: Step 1
sections.content: Cook food and leave browned bits in pan.

sections.title: Step 2
sections.content: Add liquid such as wine or stock.

title: Reduction
description: Reduction thickens sauces by evaporating liquid through simmering.

sections.title: Step 4
sections.content: Stop when sauce reaches desired thickness.

title: Emulsification
description: Emulsification combines liquids that normally do not mix such as oil and vinegar.

sections.title: Step 1
sections.content: Start with base ingredient like egg yolk or mustard.

title: Julienne Cut
description: Julienne produces thin matchstick shaped vegetable strips.

title: Brunoise Cut
description: Brunoise creates very small cubes from julienne strips.

title: Marinating
description: Marinating soaks food in seasoned liquid to add flavor and tenderize.

title: Fermentation
description: Fermentation transforms food through beneficial microorganisms.

title: Pickling
description: Pickling preserves foods in acidic liquid such as vinegar.

title: Clarified Butter
description: Clarified butter is butter that has been gently heated to remove water and milk solids, leaving pure butterfat with a higher smoke point and smooth consistency.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.content: Clarifying butter improves consistency and increases the smoke point.

sections.title: Step 1
sections.content: Melt unsalted butter slowly in a saucepan.

title: Caramelization
description: Caramelization occurs when sugars break down under heat creating deep flavor and golden color.

sections.title: Step 1
sections.content: Heat sugar or foods containing natural sugars.

sections.title: Step 2
sections.content: Allow sugars to melt and darken.

sections.title: Step 4
sections.content: Use caramelized food for deeper flavor.
